title: Helping an Elderly Parent with Mobility Issues

all.en: My father is seventy-eight years old, and his knees have grown weaker each year.
all.zh: 我父親七十八歲了，他的膝蓋一年比一年無力。

all.en: Walking up the stairs to his apartment has become a slow and painful task.
all.zh: 走樓梯回他公寓變成一件緩慢又痛苦的事。

all.en: My sister and I decided to make several changes to keep him safe at home.
all.zh: 我和姊姊決定做一些改變，讓他在家裡更安全。

all.en: First, we installed handrails along the hallway and inside the bathroom.
all.zh: 首先，我們在走廊和浴室裡裝了扶手。

all.en: We also replaced his old slippery rugs with non-slip mats in every room.
all.zh: 我們也把他舊有的滑地毯，換成每個房間都有的防滑墊。

all.en: My sister bought him a sturdy walking cane that he could use outside.
all.zh: 姊姊買了一根堅固的拐杖給他，方便他外出時使用。

all.en: At first, he refused to use it because he was afraid of looking old.
all.zh: 一開始，他不肯用，因為害怕看起來很老。

all.en: We gently reminded him that staying safe was more important than appearances.
all.zh: 我們溫和地提醒他，安全比外表重要多了。

all.en: Twice a week, a physical therapist visits to help him with simple leg exercises.
all.zh: 每週兩次，物理治療師會到府幫他做簡單的腿部運動。

all.en: On Sundays, I take him to a nearby park for a short, gentle walk.
all.zh: 每個星期天，我會帶他去附近的公園做短程的散步。

all.en: He moves slowly, but the fresh air and sunshine always lift his mood.
all.zh: 他走得很慢，但新鮮空氣和陽光總能讓他心情變好。

all.en: Caring for him has taught me patience and a deeper kind of love.
all.zh: 照顧他讓我學會了耐心，也體會到一種更深層的愛。

all.en: Last winter, I bought him a heated blanket to ease the stiffness in his legs.
all.zh: 去年冬天，我買了一條電熱毯給他，舒緩他雙腿的僵硬。

all.en: He used to refuse the elevator, but now he takes it without any complaint.
all.zh: 他以前拒絕搭電梯，但現在他會搭，不再有任何抱怨。

all.en: We installed a small chair inside the shower so he could bathe more safely.
all.zh: 我們在淋浴間裡裝了一張小椅子，讓他洗澡時更安全。

all.en: His grandchildren visit every weekend and read storybooks aloud to keep him company.
all.zh: 孫子們每個週末都來看他，朗讀故事書陪伴他。

all.en: Sometimes he gets frustrated when his legs refuse to move as fast as before.
all.zh: 有時候，當他的雙腿無法像以前一樣靈活時，他會感到沮喪。

all.en: On those days, I sit beside him and remind him of his many strengths.
all.zh: 在那些日子裡，我會坐在他身旁，提醒他身上仍擁有的許多優點。

all.en: We attached a small bell beside his bed so he could call us at night.
all.zh: 我們在他床邊裝了一個小鈴鐺，方便他晚上叫我們。

all.en: His appetite has improved since we started cooking softer and more flavorful meals.
all.zh: 自從我們開始煮較軟又較有味道的料理後，他的胃口變好了。

all.en: Last month, he walked an entire lap around the park without needing to rest.
all.zh: 上個月，他在公園繞了完整一圈，中途完全不需要休息。

all.en: Seeing his quiet smile that day was the best reward of all my efforts.
all.zh: 那天看見他靜靜的微笑，是我所有付出最棒的回報。

all.en: I installed sturdy handrails along the hallway to give him more confidence while walking.
all.zh: 我在走廊上裝了堅固的扶手，讓他走路時更有安全感。

all.en: His physical therapist praised his patience and his willingness to try difficult exercises.
all.zh: 他的物理治療師稱讚他的耐心，以及願意嘗試困難動作的態度。

all.en: On sunny afternoons, we sat together on the balcony and shared old family stories.
all.zh: 陽光普照的午後，我們一起坐在陽台上，分享老家族故事。

all.en: He told me about his childhood village, a place I had never heard of before.
all.zh: 他跟我說起他童年的村莊，那是個我從未聽過的地方。

all.en: Hearing those memories made me understand my father in a way I never had.
all.zh: 聽著那些回憶，讓我以從未有過的方式更深入地理解父親。

all.en: I bought him a comfortable cane decorated with carved wooden flowers from a local artist.
all.zh: 我買了一支舒適的拐杖給他，上面有當地藝術家雕刻的木花圖案。

all.en: He proudly carried it everywhere, often telling neighbors about its beautiful handmade details.
all.zh: 他驕傲地隨身攜帶，時常向鄰居介紹拐杖上那些美麗的手工細節。

all.en: My younger sister offered to share weekend caregiving duties so I could finally rest.
all.zh: 我妹妹主動分擔週末照護工作，讓我終於能好好休息一下。

all.en: Together we learned that caring for parents is a privilege, not just a duty.
all.zh: 我們一起體會到，照顧父母是一種榮幸，不只是責任。

all.en: Every quiet evening at home now feels like a gift wrapped in deep family love.
all.zh: 如今家中每個寧靜的夜晚，都像是被深厚家人之愛包裹的禮物。
